candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line exists, but it is generic and shown separately from both Cost of goods sold and Materials and Services, with no wording tying it specifically to COGS/COR. Conservatively avoid assigning generic operating D&A to COGS.
D&A component row under the generic depreciation section, but not labelled as COGS-related. Also inferior to the summary D&A row if any D&A were to be selected.
Component of the broader depreciation section, not specifically linked to COGS and should not be chosen over the summary row.

variables.notes: No operating row explicitly indicates depreciation/amortisation in COGS/cost of sales/materials and services. The visible D&A lines are generic operating lines, so adding them to COGS would risk misclassification and double counting.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line at the same level as the selected overhead buckets (Employee benefits expenses, Other Operating Expenses, etc.), so it appears outside the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS bucket and should be added separately. Prefer this summary row over component depreciation rows.
Looks like a component beneath the broader depreciation section; do not use when the summary D&A row is available.
Also appears to be a component of the broader D&A section; avoid if using the summary row.
Balancing/adjustment line, not appropriate as the primary D&A add-back row.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA_COMPONENTS rows do not include a total SG&A line, and the statement presents Depreciation and amortisation as a separate operating expense line above operating profit. Under the same-level separate-line rule, it should be added separately to XSGA rather than assumed embedded in the selected component bucket. No separate above-EBIT populated R&D expense row is visible, so no special R&D D&A exclusion applies here.
